accept: go test ./internal/hooks/ passes on macOS arm64 and amd64.
Fix does not regress Linux coverage.
If the test is skipped on a platform, the skip reason names the cross-platform alternative chosen for that platform.
payload.description: internal/hooks/hooks_test.go:87 hardcodes /bin/true which does not exist on macOS (canonical path is /usr/bin/true). Test reliably fails on darwin. Discovered while running 'go test ./...' as part of verification for act-a854 on 2026-05-10. Pre-existing — predates v0.2 work; verification.md showed tests green presumably because original verification ran on Linux. Fix is one of: use exec.LookPath('true'), use a compiled Go test helper as the success command, or skip the test on darwin with a t.Skip explaining the portability gap.
payload.title: internal/hooks TestRunSuccess fails on macOS: hardcoded /bin/true
